---
title: "Setup --- one-button workshop install"
---

This page gives you a **single, copy-pasteable workflow** that gets you from a fresh laptop to "ready to run Tutorial 01". Run it once, before the workshop. Re-run it any time you suspect something is broken.

::: callout-tip
**What you'll have at the end:**

1.  All required R/Bioconductor packages installed.
2.  The 20k NSCLC scRNA-seq dataset downloaded into `data/`.
3.  A clean `sessionInfo()` confirming the environment.
4.  An optional `renv` lockfile for full reproducibility.
:::

## Step 0 --- prerequisites

Make sure you already have:

-   **R ≥ 4.4** and **RStudio** (see [Software](Software.qmd)).
-   **\~6 GB of free disk space** (most of it for the dataset).
-   A working internet connection.

## Step 1 --- create a workshop project

In **RStudio**: *File → New Project → New Directory → New Project*. Name it `scrnaseq_workshop`. Inside that directory create a `data/` folder. The expected layout is:

```
scrnaseq_workshop/
├── scrnaseq_workshop.Rproj
├── data/                    # ← raw 10x output goes here (Step 3)
└── tutorials/               # ← downloaded tutorial .qmd files
```

## Step 2 --- install all packages

Paste the entire block into your R console. It will take 20--60 minutes the first time.

``` r
# CRAN
cran_pkgs <- c(
  "tidyverse", "Seurat", "remotes", "BiocManager", "patchwork",
  "presto", "harmony", "clustree", "ggrepel"
)
install.packages(setdiff(cran_pkgs, rownames(installed.packages())))

# Bioconductor
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
bioc_pkgs <- c(
  "SingleCellExperiment", "scran", "scater", "scuttle", "DropletUtils",
  "scDblFinder", "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma",
  "fgsea", "clusterProfiler",
  "SingleR", "celldex",
  "Signac", "EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86"
)
BiocManager::install(setdiff(bioc_pkgs, rownames(installed.packages())),
                     update = FALSE, ask = FALSE)

# GitHub
remotes::install_github("satijalab/seurat-data", quiet = TRUE,
                       upgrade = "never")
```

## Step 3 --- download the workshop dataset

The primary dataset is the public **10x Genomics 20k NSCLC DTC, 3' v3.1 Multiplex** release. Run this from a **terminal** (Mac/Linux/WSL), inside the project directory:

``` bash
# from inside scrnaseq_workshop/
mkdir -p data && cd data

# Filtered feature × barcode matrix (the file the tutorials read)
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-exp/6.0.0/20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex/20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex_count_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.tar.gz

tar -xzf 20k_NSCLC_DTC_3p_nextgem_Multiplex_count_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.tar.gz
```

You should end up with `data/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/` containing `barcodes.tsv.gz`, `features.tsv.gz`, and `matrix.mtx.gz`.

The bonus scATAC-seq tutorial uses a separate dataset:

``` bash
# Optional: PBMC 10k scATAC-seq v1.1 (only for the ATAC bonus)
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_filtered_peak_bc_matrix.tar.gz
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_fragments.tsv.gz
curl -L -O https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-atac/2.0.0/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem/atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_fragments.tsv.gz.tbi
tar -xzf atac_pbmc_10k_nextgem_filtered_peak_bc_matrix.tar.gz
```

## Step 4 --- verify your environment

Open a **fresh** R session in the project (so `renv` and your working directory are right) and paste:

``` r
pkgs <- c("Seurat","SingleCellExperiment","scran","scater","DropletUtils",
          "scDblFinder","harmony","DESeq2","fgsea","clusterProfiler",
          "SingleR","celldex","Signac","tidyverse","clustree","presto")
ok <- vapply(pkgs, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)

cat("Package check:\n"); print(ok)
stopifnot(all(ok))

# Read the workshop dataset
counts <- Seurat::Read10X(data.dir = "data/filtered_feature_bc_matrix")
seu    <- Seurat::CreateSeuratObject(counts = counts, project = "setup-check",
                                     min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
cat("\nSeurat object:\n"); print(seu)

cat("\nsessionInfo():\n")
sessionInfo()
```

If you see a Seurat object with \>10,000 cells, you're done.

## Step 5 (optional) --- pin your environment with `renv`

For full reproducibility (and to avoid "it worked yesterday" headaches), you can pin the exact package versions:

``` r
install.packages("renv")
renv::init()      # one time, in the project root
renv::snapshot()  # writes renv.lock — commit this with your code
```

Anyone who clones the project can then `renv::restore()` to recreate the same environment.

## Talapas users only

If you'll be running on UO's HPC instead of your laptop, see:

-   [VS Code & Talapas](Resources_Folder/VSCode_Talapas.html) --- account, login, modules, storage.
-   [SLURM Basics tutorial](Exercise_Folder/Tutorial_SLURM_Basics.html) --- interactive sessions and batch jobs.
-   [Compute Environments](Compute_Environments.qmd) --- Docker/Apptainer image and `renv` lockfile.

## Something didn't work?

See the [FAQ](FAQ.qmd) for the issues that come up most often (Mac compiler errors, `BiocManager` mirror selection, RStudio caching old library paths, "permission denied" on Talapas, etc.).
